articles.headline: Campaign Ends for High-Stakes Assembly Polls in TN and West Bengal
articles.summary: Campaigning for the Tamil Nadu assembly polls and 152 seats in West Bengal's second phase concluded on Tuesday. In Tamil Nadu, the DMK-led alliance and AIADMK-led NDA finished high-decibel outreach. In Bengal, focus remained on the Constitution Amendment Bill and women’s reservation. Voting for Bengal’s next phase is April 29, with results for multiple states expected on May 4.

articles.headline: ECI Imposes Unprecedented Nighttime Bike Ban in West Bengal
articles.summary: The Election Commission has banned motorcycle use in West Bengal between 6pm and 6am starting two days before each polling phase to prevent voter intimidation. Additionally, the ECI ordered the evacuation of tourists and outsiders from seaside resorts like Digha and Mandarmani. These measures are intended to ensure free and fair polling amid reports of sporadic political clashes.

articles.headline: PWD to Begin Expansion of Savitri and Modi Mill Flyovers
articles.summary: The Delhi Public Works Department has awarded projects to expand the Modi Mill flyover and convert the Savitri Cinema flyover into a two-way system. PWD minister Parvesh Verma stated work will begin within two months and take 24-30 months to complete. While improving long-term flow to the airport, the construction is expected to significantly impact traffic on the Outer Ring Road.

articles.headline: Thirteen Killed in Massive Fire at Thrissur Fireworks Warehouse
articles.summary: At least 13 people died and several were injured after a fire destroyed a fireworks warehouse in Mundathikode, Thrissur. The unit was preparing crackers for the upcoming Thrissur Pooram festival. The district collector has ordered a magisterial inquiry. PM Narendra Modi announced an ex-gratia of ₹2 lakh for the families of the deceased and ₹50,000 for those injured in the blast.

articles.headline: Canadian Resident Sentenced to Life for Killing Indian Student
articles.summary: Richard Jonathan Edwin was found guilty of first-degree murder for the random April 2022 killings of 21-year-old Indian student Kartik Vasudev and Elijah Eleazar Mahepath in Toronto. Vasudev was shot near a subway entrance. Despite a defence of schizophrenia, the judge ruled Edwin’s actions were planned and methodical. He has been sentenced to life imprisonment.

articles.headline: Taiwan Faces Political Opposition Over Plan to Hire Indian Workers
articles.summary: Taiwan's plan to hire Indian workers has met resistance from the opposition Kuomintang party, which cited concerns over security and the management of migrant workers. Labour Minister Hsu Ming-chun clarified that the move is aimed at addressing labour shortages in manufacturing and construction, and will only proceed with rigorous vetting and quota management.

articles.headline: Manipur Police Officer Arrested for Attacking Security Forces
articles.summary: Manipur Police arrested officer Thoudam Gojendro Singh and another resident for allegedly pelting stones at security forces during protests in Imphal East. Meanwhile, one person was injured by gunfire after a mob attempted to vandalise a government building in Thoubal where CM Y Khemchand Singh was scheduled to inaugurate a new office. Curfew has been imposed in Kangpokpi district.

articles.headline: BTS Agency Founder Bang Si-Hyuk Faces Investigation for Fraud
articles.summary: South Korean police are seeking to arrest Bang Si-Hyuk, chairman of Hybe, over allegations of a $100 million investor fraud scheme. Bang is accused of misleading investors in 2019 by claiming the agency had no plans to go public, prompting them to sell shares before an IPO. Bang's legal team denies the charges, citing full cooperation with the extended investigation.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Concludes Probe into Justice Yashwant Varma
articles.summary: A Lok Sabha-constituted inquiry committee ended its proceedings against Allahabad High Court judge Yashwant Varma following his resignation on April 9. The probe concerned allegations of unaccounted cash found at his residence. The panel decided no further action was needed as the impeachment mechanism only applies to sitting judges, though criminal proceedings remain possible.

articles.headline: Six Accused in TCS-Nashik BPO Case Seek Bail
articles.summary: Six men facing charges in the Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) Nashik BPO case have approached the court for bail. The charges include sexual harassment, hurting religious sentiments, and engaging in sexual relationships under the pretext of marriage. Seven people have been arrested in total, while one remains absconding. A court hearing for the bail pleas is scheduled for late April.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Refers Sabarimala Entry Review to Nine-Judge Bench
articles.summary: A nine-judge bench led by CJI Surya Kant questioned if the Constitution can allow permanent disqualification of believers based on birth or lineage in the Sabarimala case. The court is examining the limits of religious autonomy versus individual rights. The bench highlighted that while denominations have rights, exclusionary practices must be tested against constitutional guarantees.

articles.headline: Karnataka Congress Divided Over Action Against Minority Leaders
articles.summary: The suspension of K Abdul Jabbar and removal of Naseer Ahmed from CMO posts has caused friction in the Karnataka Congress. Minister Satish Jarkiholi and other leaders questioned the timing and alleged selective enforcement of discipline during the by-election process. Some factions claim minority leaders are being unfairly targeted despite their support being vital to the party's mandate.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Records Severe Heat as IMD Warns of Heatwave
articles.summary: Bengaluru is experiencing unusually high temperatures for April, with readings reaching 38°C, roughly 4°C above normal. The IMD predicts heatwave conditions will persist across Karnataka for another week, with some districts reaching 42°C. While scattered pre-monsoon showers may occur, they are unlikely to provide sustained relief from the dry westerly winds driving the heat.

articles.headline: Home Minister Orders Report on Internal Police Harassment Claims
articles.summary: Karnataka Home Minister G Parameshwara has sought reports from the DGP following allegations of harassment within the Directorate of Civil Rights Enforcement and Mangaluru police. Inspector TR Srinivas and SP SS Kashi alleged they were pressured by seniors to favour accused persons in atrocity cases. Parameshwara stated action would be taken based on the findings of the report.

articles.headline: Court Orders FIR Against Karnataka Home Minister Over Betting Remark
articles.summary: A Bengaluru court directed police to register an FIR against Home Minister G Parameshwara following a private complaint regarding a remark he made during a kabaddi tournament. Parameshwara jokingly mentioned losing ₹500 on a bet with a Deputy Commissioner. The court has ordered the Kodigehalli police to conduct a detailed investigation into the propriety of the statement.

articles.headline: American Woman Raped at Kodagu Homestay; Two Arrested
articles.summary: An American national was allegedly intoxicated and raped by an employee at a homestay in Kodagu, Karnataka. The victim also alleged the owner restricted her communication by disconnecting WiFi for three days to suppress the incident. Police arrested both the accused, Vrajesh Kumar, and the homestay owner. Both have been remanded to judicial custody until May 3.

articles.headline: Engineering Graduate Missing in Bengaluru After Online Extortion Threat
articles.summary: A 24-year-old engineering graduate from Odisha, Chinmay Prasad Sahu, has gone missing in Bengaluru. Police are investigating if he was abducted or fled due to mental distress after receiving extortion threats involving private photographs. Sahu left his house on April 15 and has not returned. Authorities are currently analysing digital footprints and call records.

articles.headline: Belagavi to Get ₹100-Crore Tirupati-Style Venkateshwara Temple
articles.summary: Seven acres of land have been handed over to the Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams (TTD) for the construction of a Venkateshwara Temple in Kolikoppa village, Belagavi. The project, estimated at ₹100 crore, will include accommodation and free food services for devotees, mirroring the facilities at Tirumala. Construction is expected to be completed within the next two years.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Woman Arrested for Setting Partner on Fire
articles.summary: A 27-year-old woman, Prerana, was arrested for allegedly killing her partner, Kiran, by tying him to a chair and setting him on fire in Bengaluru. Police stated the act was premeditated and driven by anger over being neglected. Prerana reportedly recorded the act on her mobile phone and attempted to mislead investigators by claiming the victim set himself on fire.

articles.headline: I-T Dept Flags Inconsistencies in Udhayanidhi Stalin’s Assets
articles.summary: The Income Tax Department informed the Madras High Court that a ₹7.36 crore investment disclosed by Tamil Nadu Deputy CM Udhayanidhi Stalin in 2021 is missing from his 2026 affidavit. The department also noted discrepancies in loan disclosures and company borrowings. While no conclusive findings have been made, the court was asked for more time to verify financial records.

articles.headline: Telangana RTC Workers Launch Strike Over Pay and Merger
articles.summary: Over 6,000 Telangana State Road Transport Corporation (TGSRTC) buses stayed off roads as employees began a strike for demands including pay revision and merger with the state transport department. The government has formed a high-level committee to examine the issues and submit a report within four weeks, urging unions to avoid causing public inconvenience.

articles.headline: Cash Snatched from Election Officials at Karti Chidambaram’s Office
articles.summary: Two unidentified men snatched ₹13.40 lakh in seized cash from election flying squad officials at the Karaikudi office of Congress MP Karti Chidambaram. The attackers also stole the keys to the officials' vehicle to prevent a chase. Police have arrested one local functionary and are searching for the others involved in the dacoity and obstruction of public servants.

articles.headline: ED Mounts Over a Dozen Raids Ahead of West Bengal Polls
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has conducted high-profile raids and arrests in West Bengal over the past month, targeting I-PAC and senior police officials. I-PAC director Vinesh Chandel was arrested on April 13 in a money laundering case. The TMC has accused the BJP of weaponising federal agencies to disrupt its election campaign through intimidation and asset attachments.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court Directs Processing of Pernia Qureshi’s Visa
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has directed the FRRO to decide on the visa application of Pernia Qureshi, daughter of meat exporter Moin Akhtar Qureshi, within 10 days. Pernia, a U.S. citizen, had challenged the rejection of her OCI status based on her mother’s former Pakistani citizenship. The court noted she has already surrendered her Pakistani citizenship and has been an Indian citizen since 1995.

articles.headline: Security Beefed Up for First Anniversary of Pahalgam Terror Attack
articles.summary: Kashmir authorities have increased security and opened 39 of 48 tourist spots closed after last year's Pahalgam attack that killed 26 people. A memorial has been erected in Pahalgam to honour the victims. While tourism is slowly returning with 30-40% hotel occupancy, sites like Baisaran remain heavily guarded by CRPF to ensure the safety of domestic and international visitors.

articles.headline: Rajnath Singh Says West Asia War Has Direct Fallout for India
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh stated in Berlin that the US-Israel-Iran conflict impacts India's energy and food security. He noted that disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are a direct threat to India's stability. Singh is visiting Germany to sign a defence industrial cooperation roadmap and strengthen strategic partnerships in AI, cybersecurity, and drone technology.

articles.headline: Gangster Lawrence Bishnoi to Face MCOCA Charges in Delhi
articles.summary: A Delhi court ordered the framing of charges under the stringent Maharashtra Control of Organised Crimes Act (MCOCA) against Lawrence Bishnoi and 19 associates. The group is accused of running an organised crime syndicate involved in contract killings and extortion. Bishnoi will remain in Sabarmati jail due to security concerns and will appear via video conferencing.

articles.headline: Two Indian Landfills Among Top 25 Global Methane Emitters
articles.summary: A report by UCLA identified waste facilities in Secunderabad (Telangana) and Mumbai (Maharashtra) as being among the top 25 methane emitters globally. The Secunderabad site reportedly emits 5.9 tonnes of methane per hour. Experts warned that these emissions pose significant risks to public health and contribute heavily to global warming, second only to carbon dioxide.

articles.headline: US Navy Seizes Oil Tanker in Campaign to Disrupt Iran Network
articles.summary: US forces conducted a maritime interdiction of the stateless vessel M/T Tifani in the Indian Ocean, marking the first such intervention since the Iranian blockade began. The Pentagon stated international waters are not a refuge for sanctioned vessels providing support to Iran. The action signals increased pressure on the 'dark fleet' of non-Iranian flagged ships amid the regional conflict.

articles.headline: Nasa Curiosity Rover Discovers Organic Building Blocks on Mars
articles.summary: Nasa’s Curiosity rover has detected over 20 organic molecules, including benzothiophene, in Mars' Gale crater. While not definitive proof of past life, the findings show that prebiotic chemical building blocks have been preserved on the planet's surface for over three billion years. This experiment, using TMAH chemical analysis, was the first of its kind conducted on another planet.

articles.headline: Four Foreign Coaches Appointed to Guide Indian Wrestling Team
articles.summary: The Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) has hired four foreign coaches from Georgia, Russia, Japan, and the USA to train the national team. The move aims to centralise training within national camps rather than allowing wrestlers to train independently overseas. The coaches, including personal trainers of past medallists, are tasked with preparing the squad for the 2026 Asian Games.

articles.headline: Twenty-Eight NSE Companies Spend on CSR Despite Average Net Losses
articles.summary: Data shows 28 NSE-listed companies continued corporate social responsibility (CSR) spending in FY25 despite posting average net losses over the past three years. Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles led the group with a ₹26.3 crore CSR expenditure despite an average loss of ₹462.6 crore. Other major spenders include Aditya Birla Fashion and Retail, India Cements, and PVR Inox.

articles.headline: Donald Trump Expresses Confidence in Securing Negotiated Deal with Iran
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the U.S. is in a strong negotiating position and believes Iran has no choice but to reach a deal. The statement comes as high-level negotiations are expected to resume in Pakistan. Tensions remain high following U.S. naval activity in the Indian Ocean and a fragile ceasefire in the Middle East that is nearing its expiration date.

articles.headline: Indian Investors Remain Resilient Despite Equity Market Turbulence in March
articles.summary: Indian mutual fund investors demonstrated long-term commitment in March 2026, with equity-oriented schemes recording ₹40,450 crore in net inflows despite an 11% fall in the BSE Sensex. SIP contributions reached a record ₹32,087 crore. Domestic institutions provided a cushion against volatility as foreign portfolio investors liquidated ₹1.17 trillion during the same period.

articles.headline: Government to Launch Health Claims Index to Increase Insurance Transparency
articles.summary: The Centre plans to build a public 'Health Claims Index' using data from the National Health Claims Exchange (NHCX). The index aims to offer consumers visibility into insurer claim handling, approval rates, and cost patterns. By standardizing clinical coding and treatment protocols, the government hopes to reduce billing discrepancies and detect fraud in the paper-heavy industry.

articles.headline: HCL Technologies Forecasts Slower Growth Following Record FY26 Revenue
articles.summary: HCL Technologies reported a 5.95% revenue growth to $14.66 billion for FY26, its fastest in three years. However, net profit fell 4.05% to $1.96 billion. The company issued a cautious revenue growth guidance of 1-4% for the current fiscal year, citing an uncertain demand environment and client-specific challenges in North America and Europe amid global macroeconomic pressures.

articles.headline: Private Equity Firms Evaluate Stake in Jewellery Brand Giva
articles.summary: Multiples Private Equity, Warburg Pincus, and Kedaara Capital are reportedly evaluating an $80-100 million stake in jewellery brand Giva. The company is seeking a valuation of over ₹6,000 crore. The funding round is expected to be 75% primary capital for expansion, including new stores and lab-grown diamond inventory, with a secondary component allowing early investors to exit.

articles.headline: Indian Consumer Demand Softens Amid Inflation and Weak Monsoon Outlook
articles.summary: India's Index of Consumer Sentiments edged down 0.2% in March as rising essential costs squeezed lower-income budgets. Affluent households also reported a 6% decline in expectations due to global conflicts and stagnant hiring. While essentials like induction cookers saw growth due to LPG price hikes, big-ticket discretionary spending on electronics and appliances has begun to cool.

articles.headline: India's Foodgrain Stocks Triple Buffer Norms Following Robust Procurement
articles.summary: Central pool foodgrain reserves reached 60.48 million tonnes as of 1 April, nearly three times the mandatory buffer. Wheat stocks rose 85% year-on-year to 21.79 million tonnes, while rice reached 38.61 million tonnes. Experts and industry stakeholders are urging the government to proactively offload excess stocks in international markets to stabilize prices and manage storage costs.

articles.headline: UN Projects India’s GDP Growth to Moderate to 6.4% in FY27
articles.summary: The United Nations Unescap report projects India’s GDP growth will slow to 6.4% in FY27 from 7.4% in FY26 due to West Asia conflicts and energy supply disruptions. Inflation is expected to rise to 4.4% in FY27. Growth is projected to pick up moderately to 6.6% in FY28, driven by domestic demand. Developing countries in Asia are broadly expected to see slower growth in 2026.

articles.headline: Government to Use Grassroots Workers for Nationwide Digital Fraud Drive
articles.summary: India is planning a financial literacy drive involving 1.05 million ASHA workers and 1.3 million Anganwadi staff to curb digital scams like 'digital arrest' frauds. The initiative includes AI-based transaction monitoring to block suspicious transfers in real time. Cybercrime losses reached ₹44,000 crore over 2024-2025, prompting the need for community-level awareness and safeguards.

articles.headline: India Losing Over Nine Billion Dollars Annually to Textile Waste
articles.summary: A report by Ficci and the Resource Efficiency and Circular Economy Industry Coalition states India loses $9.4 billion in value annually due to gaps in textile waste collection and recycling. The country generates 7.25 million tonnes of textile waste every year. Improved sorting and recycling systems are needed to recover this value and enhance resource efficiency.

articles.headline: India Seafood Exports Reach Record Levels in Fiscal Year 2025-2026
articles.summary: India's seafood exports grew 11.2% to $8.28 billion (19.32 lakh metric tonnes) in FY26, driven by demand from Europe and China. While value rose, exports to the U.S. dipped 14.15% to $2.32 billion. The government noted that prices of dairy products remain stable with no current supply interruptions despite regional conflicts in West Asia affecting some logistics.

articles.headline: Solar Cell Shortage Looming as New Local Sourcing Mandates Approach
articles.summary: The North India Module Manufacturer Association warned of a severe solar cell shortage starting in June due to new rules mandating the use of locally made cells. The association stated that the mandate would drive up module prices and delay projects across the pipeline. They have requested the renewable energy ministry to review the timeline to prevent disruptions in the solar sector.

articles.headline: Postal Department Revenue Rises 16% to Over Fifteen Thousand Crore
articles.summary: The Department of Posts recorded revenue of ₹15,296 crore for FY26, a 16% year-on-year growth. Communications Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia stated that the department is targeting higher growth in FY27. Separately, the government has formed a four-member team led by M. Venkatesh to investigate a recent fire incident at the HPCL Rajasthan Refinery project in Barmer.

articles.headline: Renewable Platform Evren Secures $600 Million from Global Lender Consortium
articles.summary: Brookfield-backed renewable energy platform Evren has secured approximately $600 million from a consortium of lenders including Standard Chartered, MUFG, and DBS Bank. The funding will support India’s next-generation energy infrastructure. The deal reflects growing global lender confidence in the Indian renewable sector despite broader macroeconomic uncertainties.

articles.headline: SpaceX Holds Closed-Door Analyst Meetings Ahead of Massive IPO
articles.summary: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is hosting top Wall Street analysts this week for three days of meetings at its Texas and Tennessee facilities. The company is reportedly looking to raise $75 billion in an initial public offering (IPO) that could be the world’s biggest ever. Executives are targeting a late June trading debut, marking one of the most anticipated market entries in history.

articles.headline: Tea Cafe Chain Chaayos Appoints Avendus to Raise Seventy Million Dollars
articles.summary: Chaayos has appointed Avendus Capital to advise on a $50-70 million funding round. The round is expected to include a $20 million secondary component as Tiger Global seeks to exit its stake. The capital will be used for expansion in India's food services market. Chaayos reported operating revenue of ₹310.6 crore in FY25, up from ₹248.6 crore in the previous year.

articles.headline: Companies Raise Prices to Shield Margins Against Crude Oil Inflation
articles.summary: Multiple sectors, including cement, paints, and FMCG, are implementing price hikes to combat rising input costs linked to elevated crude oil prices. Pan-India cement prices rose ₹10-12 per bag in April, while Asian Paints announced cumulative high single-digit increases. While hikes protect margins, experts warn that sustained inflation could lead to moderated volume growth.

articles.headline: Infra.Market Founders Take Personal Debt to Support Company Capital
articles.summary: The founders of Tiger Global-backed Infra.Market are reportedly taking personal debt through their promoter entity, Silverline Homes, to inject fresh capital into the firm. The move comes as market volatility delays the company’s planned ₹5,000 crore IPO. The infusion is aimed at improving the company’s debt-to-equity ratio and shoring up the balance sheet before testing public markets.

articles.headline: Sebi’s F&O Curbs Reduce Small Trader Participation by 25 Percent
articles.summary: Measures by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) to cool retail frenzy in options trading are showing results. NSE data shows the number of investors trading less than ₹10,000 fell by 40% to 450,000 in FY26. Total small trader participation dropped nearly 25%, as the regulator imposed higher margins and restricted broker funding to curb excessive speculation.

articles.headline: Corporate Bond Fundraising Falls 9% as Rising Yields Shift Demand
articles.summary: Funds raised through private placement of listed corporate bonds fell 9% to ₹8.99 trillion in FY26. A sharp rise in government bond yields diminished the cost advantage of bonds over bank loans. Additionally, heavy supply of State Development Loans (SDLs) crowded out corporate borrowers, leading many firms and NBFCs to turn to scheduled commercial banks for funding requirements.

articles.headline: Indian Smartphone Lifespan Hits Record Four Years as Prices Soar
articles.summary: The average smartphone usage cycle in India has reached nearly 48 months as rising prices and incremental hardware gains delay upgrades. The average device cost has risen to over ₹26,000. While premium sales grew 20% on Amazon, many middle-market buyers are opting for repairs or refurbished units. Analysts project a total smartphone market volume dip of up to 8% in 2026.

articles.headline: Delivery Platform Borzo Targets B2B Growth Amid Quick Commerce Competition
articles.summary: Intracity delivery platform Borzo is shifting its focus from individuals to mid-market and large enterprises to rebalance its client mix. India accounts for 65% of Borzo's gross merchandise value. The company, which has been profitable in India for two years, aims for a 50-25-25 split across small businesses, mid-market firms, and enterprises while maintaining a stable rider base.

articles.headline: Mirae to Manage Six Thousand Crore India Fund for Krafton and Naver
articles.summary: South Korean tech giants Krafton and Naver have launched a ₹6,000 crore India-focused fund to back early and growth-stage startups. Mirae Asset Venture Investments India will manage the Unicorn Growth Fund, which will target AI, space-tech, and consumer brands. The fund plans to write cheques of $10-35 million for Series B and C rounds, focusing on vertical tech platforms.

articles.headline: Trai Spam Control Plan Sparks Conflict with Banks and Fintech Firms
articles.summary: Trai's proposal to use AI for automatically penalizing spam senders has drawn opposition from HDFC Bank, PhonePe, and DHL. Lenders warn that automated systems could mistakenly block essential alerts like OTPs and fraud warnings. While spam complaints tripled to 2.5 million, platforms argue a 'one-size-fits-all' approach risks disrupting the digital backbone of the economy.

articles.headline: Noida International Airport Launch Faces Uncertainty Over CEO Security Clearance
articles.summary: The mid-May launch of Noida International Airport faces potential delays as CEO Christoph Schnellmann awaits mandatory security clearance from the Bureau of Civil Aviation Security (BCAS). As a Swiss national, Schnellmann's appointment requires specific home ministry inputs for security coordination roles. The ₹11,200 crore first phase is designed to handle 12 million passengers annually.

articles.headline: Asian Oil Buyers Struggle as Hormuz Strait Alternatives Shrink
articles.summary: Asia’s largest oil importers, India and China, are facing an energy crunch as floating supplies of Russian and Iranian oil diminish. Global supply shrank 10% last month due to the blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. While India has secured some safe passage for LPG, refiners are bracing for widespread fuel price hikes as domestic stockpiles run low and shipping costs escalate.

articles.headline: Fed Nominee Kevin Warsh Vows Independent Monetary Policy from Politics
articles.summary: Federal Reserve chief nominee Kevin Warsh told U.S. senators he would make interest rate decisions independent of pressure from President Donald Trump. Warsh argued that the Fed's 'plot armour' is its commitment to low inflation. While Trump has called for quick rate cuts, Warsh faces a Senate hearing to detail how he will manage the central bank amid persistent global energy shocks.

articles.headline: Japan Eases Weapons Export Restrictions in Major Post-War Policy Shift
articles.summary: Japan has approved changes to defense export rules, allowing the nation to sell lethal military equipment overseas for the first time since World War II. The move, led by Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, aims to strengthen Japan's defense industrial base. The policy change follows a deal to sell warships to Australia and reflects growing regional security concerns regarding China and North Korea.

articles.headline: Adani Power Forms Nuclear Energy Subsidiary for Domestic Generation
articles.summary: Adani Power has incorporated a new subsidiary, Rawatbhata-Raj Atomic Energy Ltd, to engage in the generation and distribution of electricity from nuclear sources. The move aligns with the government's target of reaching 100GW of nuclear capacity by 2047. The subsidiary was formed under Adani Atomic Energy Ltd to explore private participation in India's expanding atomic energy sector.

articles.headline: Posco Set to Re-Enter Odisha Through Joint Venture with JSW
articles.summary: South Korean steel giant Posco is returning to Odisha over 20 years after abandoning a mega project due to local protests. The company signed an agreement to set up two major greenfield plants in Keonjhar and Dhenkanal districts in collaboration with JSW Group. The projects include a 5mtpa and a 6mtpa capacity mill, marking a significant return of foreign direct investment to the state.

articles.headline: Analysis: Tamil Nadu’s Dravidian Duopoly Faces Four-Cornered Electoral Challenge
articles.summary: The upcoming 2026 Tamil Nadu assembly elections face a shift as actor Joseph Vijay’s TVK and Seeman’s NTK challenge the dominance of DMK and AIADMK. While the Stalin-led DMK government reports strong 11.2% GSDP growth and social welfare success, anti-incumbency and a four-cornered contest have made the outcome unpredictable. The entry of new parties is especially wooing younger voters.

articles.headline: Indian Home Loan Seekers Face Documentation and Approval Delays
articles.summary: Despite a home loan boom, 43% of Indian borrowers cite excessive documentation as a major hurdle. A survey of 7,400 individuals found that 17% face prolonged approval timelines. Issues include repeated document submissions and lack of transparency regarding registered mortgages. Experts suggest greater use of DigiLocker and standardized checklists to improve the borrowing experience.

articles.headline: Quantum Computing Advances Threaten Current Digital Encryption Standards
articles.summary: Recent breakthroughs in quantum computing by Google and Caltech suggest that traditional cryptographic protections like RSA and ECC may be vulnerable sooner than expected. India's Digital Public Infrastructure, including Aadhaar and UPI, relies on these standards. The government has set a 2029 target for full migration to post-quantum cyber readiness to protect national data security.

articles.headline: China’s Pharmaceutical Innovation Sector Approaches Global Dominance
articles.summary: China has overtaken the US in the number of early-stage drug research programmes, with over 6,000 active projects in 2024. In 2025, China launched more novel medicines than any other country. Supported by public funding and a vast patient pool for clinical trials, Chinese firms signed a record $133 billion in licensing deals last year, signaling a shift in the global pharma order.

articles.headline: US and Iran Signal New Ceasefire Talks in Islamabad
articles.summary: Regional officials confirm the US and Iran will participate in a new round of ceasefire talks in Islamabad. US President Donald Trump warned he is prepared to resume bombing if negotiations do not progress. Delegations will be led by US VP J.D. Vance and Iranian Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. The move comes as a two-week ceasefire is set to expire.

articles.headline: Stock Markets Reach Six-Week High as V-Shaped Recovery Continues
articles.summary: Indian markets witnessed a sharp recovery similar to the post-COVID period. The Sensex jumped 753 points to close at 79,273, while the Nifty rose 212 points to 24,577. The BSE 500 index has climbed 12.1% this month, the best monthly bounce since April 2020. Investors are viewing recent dips as buying opportunities despite geopolitical risks.

articles.headline: Rupee Slips to 93.50 Against Dollar Following RBI Easing
articles.summary: The Indian rupee weakened by 0.4% to close at 93.50 per dollar after the RBI partially eased restrictions on bank participation in the derivative arbitrage trade. The move allowed onshore-offshore price gaps to be exploited by traders. Analysts expect the currency to trade between 93.00 and 93.75 in the near term as global oil prices add pressure.

articles.headline: Life Insurance New Business Premium Crosses Rs 4 Lakh Crore
articles.summary: The life insurance industry recorded a 15.7% growth in New Business Premium (NBP) for FY26, surpassing the Rs 4 lakh crore milestone for the first time to reach Rs 4.59 lakh crore. LIC's premium grew 14.9% to Rs 2.60 lakh crore, while private insurers saw a 16.75% rise to Rs 1.99 lakh crore. Market normalization and new product launches drove the surge.

articles.headline: Government Proposes Strict Disclosure Norms for AI-Generated Content
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and IT has proposed new rules requiring mandatory and visible identification marks on all content created using Artificial Intelligence. The revised draft shifts from 'prominent display' to continuous visible markers throughout videos or visuals. The regulations will also apply to independent news content creators.

articles.headline: Indian Exporters Eye $12 Billion Share in US Tariff Refund
articles.summary: Following a US court ruling that certain tariffs imposed under the Trump administration were illegal, a $166 billion refund has been announced. Indian exporters, primarily in the textiles and apparel sectors, are eligible for an estimated $12 billion share. While legal official importers in the US will receive the funds, Indian firms hope to negotiate shares.

articles.headline: Hilton and Olive Hospitality to Launch 10 Spark Hotels
articles.summary: Hilton and Olive Hospitality signed an agreement to open 10 'Spark by Hilton' hotels across India. The hotels will be located in key business and travel hubs including Bengaluru, Goa, Jaipur, and Pune. This marks Spark's debut in the Indian market, targeting the growing mid-scale segment and urban development beyond five-star categories.

articles.headline: Navrakriti to Invest Rs 100 Crore in Odisha Battery Recycling
articles.summary: Battery recycling firm Navrakriti announced plans to invest over Rs 100 crore to set up a new rare minerals refining unit in Odisha. Expected to be operational by FY29, the facility will extract cobalt, nickel, and lithium. The project aligns with the National Critical Minerals Mission to reduce import dependence and boost local sourcing for battery production.

articles.headline: Flipkart eKart Expands Partnership with IKEA to Chennai
articles.summary: Flipkart’s logistics arm eKart is expanding its partnership with furniture giant IKEA to provide last-mile delivery services in Chennai. The service will utilize eKart's electric vehicle fleet for eco-friendly deliveries. Chennai becomes the second major market for this collaboration after Delhi-NCR, leveraging eKart's tech-enabled supply chain for heavy goods.

articles.headline: Biocon Receives Health Canada Approval for Bone Disease Drugs
articles.summary: Biocon Limited has received approval from Health Canada for its biological drugs Yesafili and Wezlana (denosumab). These drugs are used to treat osteoporosis and bone complications related to cancer. The company received the Notice of Compliance on April 3, 2026, marking a significant entry into the Canadian pharmaceutical market for these biosimilars.

articles.headline: Welspun One Leases Warehouse Space to Invivo India at JNPA
articles.summary: Welspun One has leased 2.10 lakh square feet of warehouse and logistics space at the JNPA Special Economic Zone to Invivo India. The facility will serve as a strategic hub for Invivo to expand its 'Free Trade Warehousing Zone' (FTWZ) capabilities. The partnership aims to improve trade flow management and provide integrated supply chain solutions for global trade.

articles.headline: Smartworks Portfolio Crosses 1 Crore Square Feet Landmark
articles.summary: Flexible workspace provider Smartworks announced its operational portfolio has surpassed 1 crore square feet, becoming the first listed company in this segment to reach the milestone. The growth was achieved after the opening of the Tata Intelligent Park center in Mumbai. The company noted a shift in demand towards managed campus-style solutions for large enterprises.

articles.headline: Persistent Systems Q4 Profit Jumps 33.7% to Rs 529 Crore
articles.summary: Persistent Systems reported a 33.7% year-on-year increase in net profit to Rs 529.2 crore for Q4 FY26. Revenue rose 25% to Rs 4,055.9 crore compared to the same period last year. The company attributed its performance to continuous demand for AI-led services and a stable business model. Annual revenue for FY26 grew 11.18% to Rs 13,014.4 crore.

articles.headline: Pharma and Healthcare Firms Expect Stable Q4 Revenue Growth
articles.summary: Indian pharmaceutical and healthcare companies are expected to report stable Q4 FY26 revenue growth of 8-10%. While generic exports to the US face pricing pressure, domestic demand remains strong, particularly in chronic disease categories which saw 16.5% growth. Sun Pharma, Zydus Lifesciences, and Ajanta Pharma are projected to be among the top performers.

articles.headline: Oberoi Realty Q4 Bookings Surge 96% to Rs 1,673 Crore
articles.summary: Oberoi Realty reported a 96% increase in Q4 FY26 booking value to Rs 1,673 crore, driven by high demand for premium real estate in Mumbai. The booked area rose 160% to 3,57,552 square feet. For the full fiscal year FY26, the developer recorded total bookings of Rs 5,447 crore, a 3.14% increase over the previous year despite geopolitical uncertainties.

articles.headline: Hindustan Copper Plans Rs 7,189 Crore Capex Over Five Years
articles.summary: State-run Hindustan Copper (HCL) has planned a capital expenditure of Rs 7,188.9 crore over the next five years to expand mine production. The goal is to increase ore production from 42.1 lakh tonnes in FY26 to 1.22 crore tonnes by FY30. The company also launched its 'IT Vision 2026-2030' to transform into a digitally integrated enterprise.

articles.headline: India Leads Global Adoption of AI in Healthcare Services
articles.summary: A BCG study shows India leads the world in AI adoption for healthcare, with 85% of consumers using AI-based tools for personal health. Indian consumers use AI more than those in the US (50%) and UK (43%) for symptom checking and interpreting medical results. The study highlights a shift towards 'Agentic AI' for managing appointments and pharmacy needs.

articles.headline: Sovereign Wealth Funds Maintain Indian Holdings Amid Iran Conflict
articles.summary: Analysis of FPI data shows Sovereign Wealth Funds (SWFs) maintained their stake in India at 6.5% in March, up slightly from 6.4% in February. This stability persists despite overall FPI outflows of Rs 1.2 lakh crore from equities in March. SWFs are shifting focus from energy to diversified sectors, showing long-term confidence in Indian economic stability.

articles.headline: Sage Financial Acquires Algo Trading Platform Strateji
articles.summary: Sage Financial Services has acquired Algorithmic trading platform Strateji in a cash and stock deal. The acquisition will help Sage expand its technology-based services for active traders and investors. Strateji offers over 100 multi-asset algorithms across equity, index, derivatives, and commodity segments, enhancing Sage's fintech portfolio.

articles.headline: Life Insurance Stocks Tumble After Government Remarks on Tie-ups
articles.summary: Shares of listed life insurance companies fell after DFS Secretary M.P. Nagaraju suggested banks should avoid exclusive partnerships with their subsidiary insurers and remain neutral. SBI Life dropped 3.32% and Canara HSBC Life fell 4.36%. Analysts believe a shift toward an 'open architecture' model would increase distribution costs and impact market share for bank-backed insurers.

articles.headline: Hindalco Likely to Replace Trent in BSE Sensex Index
articles.summary: Metals major Hindalco Industries is expected to replace Trent in the BSE Sensex during the June 2026 reshuffle. Hindalco's stock has risen 60% over the last year, improving its ranking and free-float market capitalization. Conversely, Trent has faced selling pressure, dropping its rank and making it vulnerable to exclusion from the 30-share benchmark index.

articles.headline: Renewable Energy Production Surges While Fossil Fuel Use Declines
articles.summary: India's renewable energy production increased by 98 terawatt-hours (TWh) in 2025, according to the Global Electricity Review. Simultaneously, fossil fuel-based production fell by 52 TWh. This marks a significant shift as 2025 became the first century where both India and China saw a decline in fossil fuel power generation due to a massive scale-up of green energy sources.

articles.headline: India Becomes Second Largest Global Wind Energy Market
articles.summary: India surpassed the US and Germany to become the world's second-largest wind energy market in 2025, trailing only China. According to BloombergNEF, India installed a record 6.3 gigawatts of wind capacity last year, an 85% increase from 2024. Adani Wind rose to 15th place globally among turbine manufacturers, installing over 1 GW at its Khavda renewable energy park.

articles.headline: Pakistan Extends Ban on Indian Flights Until May 24
articles.summary: Pakistan's Aviation Authority has extended the ban on Indian registered, chartered, and commercial aircraft using its airspace until May 24, 2026. The restriction was originally imposed following regional tensions. The 'Notice to Airmen' confirms that no Indian flights will be permitted to enter Pakistani flight regions during this extended period.

articles.headline: UN ESCAP Lowers India's FY26 Growth Forecast to 6.4%
articles.summary: UN ESCAP has cut India's growth forecast for FY2026 to 6.4% from its previous estimate of 6.6%. The report cites external risks including the Middle East conflict, rising commodity prices, and supply chain disruptions. Inflation is projected to reach 4.4% in 2026. These factors are expected to weaken global demand and impact India's exports and investment sentiment.

articles.headline: Fertilizer Production Hits 13-Year Low as Natural Gas Supplies Stall
articles.summary: India's fertilizer production fell by 0.1% in FY26, the weakest performance in 13 years. Output dropped sharply in March 2026 due to LNG supply interruptions caused by the West Asia crisis and annual maintenance shutdowns. Urea production fell 27% in March compared to the previous year, though officials expect a recovery in April as gas availability improves.

articles.headline: Moody's Cuts India FY27 GDP Growth Projection to 6%
articles.summary: Moody's Ratings has lowered India's FY2027 real GDP growth forecast to 6% from 6.8%. The agency cited high fuel costs due to the Middle East war, increased input costs, and slowing private consumption. As a major importer of crude and LPG, India remains highly sensitive to global energy price hikes, which are widening the trade deficit and impacting industrial activity.

articles.headline: India Opens First Platinum Group Metals Recovery Plant in Odisha
articles.summary: Odisha Mining Corporation (OMC) has launched the country's first integrated pilot plant for the extraction and refining of Platinum Group Metals (PGM) in Bhubaneswar. The Rs 10 crore project aims to secure domestic supplies of strategic minerals like platinum, palladium, and rhodium. These metals are critical for clean energy technologies, electronics, and medical equipment.

articles.headline: Tax Experts Seek Clarity on Broadened 'Export Incentive' Definition
articles.summary: Tax professionals have asked the CBDT for urgent clarification on the scope of 'Export Incentives' under the new Income Tax Act, 2025. Experts fear the broad terminology in Section 26 could lead to the taxation of indirect or hypothetical gains, moving beyond standard scrap-based benefits and RoDTEP, potentially creating new litigation for exporters.

articles.headline: Delhi Bullion Market: Silver and Gold Prices Advance
articles.summary: Silver prices in Delhi rose by Rs 700 to Rs 2.58 lakh per kilogram. Gold prices also increased by Rs 300 to Rs 1,57,300 per 10 grams. In global markets, silver fell 1.29% to $78.69 per ounce, while gold dipped nearly 1% to $4,776.85 per ounce, reflecting localized demand despite global fluctuations.

articles.headline: Poor States Report Higher Treatment Costs in Government Hospitals
articles.summary: An NSO survey revealed that government hospital treatment is more expensive in poorer states like Bihar (Rs 10,553), UP (Rs 12,878), and Jharkhand (Rs 12,364) compared to the national average of Rs 6,631. In contrast, wealthy Tamil Nadu reported costs of just Rs 1,357. The disparity is attributed to differences in medicine availability, staff strength, and out-of-pocket spending.

articles.headline: Amit Shah Urges Fearless Voting in Bengal Rallies
articles.summary: Home Minister Amit Shah called on voters in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu to vote without fear in the first phase of assembly elections on April 23. He announced the deployment of a record 2.5 lakh central security personnel to monitor miscreants. Shah pledged to seal the Bangladesh border within 45 days if the BJP comes to power in Bengal.

articles.headline: India and South Korea Sign Agreement for Shipbuilding Cooperation
articles.summary: India and South Korea have signed the 'VOYAGES' agreement to boost cooperation in shipbuilding and maritime efficiency. Under the pact, HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering will help design and build next-generation autonomous vessels in India. The agreement aims to reduce India's 90% dependence on China for port cranes and improve shipyard scale.

articles.headline: India-South Korea Target $50 Billion Trade by 2030
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and South Korean President Lee Jae-myung have set a target to increase bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030. Despite a widening trade deficit for India, both nations committed to expanding cooperation in strategic sectors. India encouraged Korean firms to register ships in Gujarat's GIFT IFSC to benefit from local financial incentives.

articles.headline: Gadkari pitches for flex fuel vehicles to curb oil imports
articles.summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari urged the auto industry to adopt flex-fuel vehicles capable of running on up to 100% ethanol. The move aims to achieve self-reliance and reduce India's Rs 22 lakh crore oil import bill. The government is currently working on a notification to facilitate the rollout of E100-compatible vehicles across the country.

articles.headline: For Gadchiroli Naxals, a move back to mainstream life
articles.summary: Lloyds Metals is providing jobs, equity ownership, and healthcare to over 80 former Naxalites in Maharashtra’s Gadchiroli district. The company has invested Rs 6,000 crore in the region, with plans for another Rs 25,000 crore in ongoing projects. The initiative aims to integrate former militants into the workforce and improve local social infrastructure.

articles.headline: HCLTech lowers FY27 revenue growth guidance to 1-4%
articles.summary: HCLTech projected revenue growth of 1-4% for FY27, down from the previous 2-5%, citing market volatility and reduced discretionary spending. The company's Q4 net profit rose 4.2% year-on-year to Rs 4,488 crore. CEO C Vijayakumar noted that while the deal pipeline remains strong, the conversion to revenue has slowed due to cautious client spending.

articles.headline: Cyient DLM Q4 profit falls 28% to Rs 22 crore
articles.summary: Cyient DLM reported a 27.7% decline in consolidated net profit to Rs 22.44 crore for the fourth quarter. However, revenue from operations grew by 13.8% to Rs 369.07 crore. The company, which specializes in electronic manufacturing services, continues to expand its manufacturing capacity and diversify its global client base.

articles.headline: Ex-MRPL MD-led team probes Barmer refinery fire incident
articles.summary: The Oil Ministry has appointed a four-member team, led by a former MRPL MD, to investigate the fire at the Rajasthan Refinery in Barmer. Preliminary reports suggest a hydrocarbon leak from a valve caused the blaze. Commissioning of the refinery may face delays depending on the investigation's findings. All units are currently safe and unaffected.

articles.headline: EQT raises $15.6 b in largest Asia-focused buyout fund
articles.summary: Swedish private equity firm EQT has closed BPEA IX, raising $15.6 billion, its largest Asia-focused buyout fund to date. The fund will target investments in technology, healthcare, and industrial sectors across the region. The successful fundraise highlights investor confidence in Asian markets despite a general global slowdown in private equity.

articles.headline: Tech Mahindra Q4 margins expected to grow 50-70 bps
articles.summary: Analysts expect Tech Mahindra's EBIT margin to improve by 50-70 basis points in Q4, supported by cost optimization through its 'Project Fortius' program. While revenue growth is expected to remain flat, the company aims to achieve a 15% EBIT margin by FY27. Market focus remains on the company's ability to drive efficiency and expand its AI-led service offerings.

articles.headline: Drivin raises $140 m for electric truck and bus fleet
articles.summary: Electric mobility startup Drivin has raised $140 million in a funding round led by Nomura. The Gurugram-based firm plans to deploy 300 electric buses and trucks on major routes, focusing on corridor-based charging infrastructure. Drivin aims to build a vehicle asset base worth over Rs 1,200 crore to accelerate the adoption of heavy electric vehicles.

articles.headline: Trump threatens to bomb Iran if no deal by Wednesday
articles.summary: Former US President Donald Trump stated he would authorize bombing Iran if a deal with the US is not reached before a week-long ceasefire ends this Wednesday. Trump criticized the current administration's handling of foreign policy and suggested his approach would be more decisive. He also mentioned a willingness to negotiate under different terms.

articles.headline: Trump says he is considering currency swap with UAE
articles.summary: Donald Trump mentioned he is considering a currency swap with the UAE to help the West Asian ally secure US dollars amid regional economic shifts. He emphasized that maintaining the dollar's status as a global reserve currency is a priority. The move is seen as an effort to support strategic allies while protecting US financial interests.

articles.headline: Nifty gains for third session; FMCG and realty lead
articles.summary: The Nifty rose for the third consecutive day, gaining 50 points to end at 24,576.60, while the Sensex surged 753.03 points to 79,273.33. Gains were supported by positive global cues, easing oil prices, and the start of the Q4 earnings season. FMCG and realty sectors were the top performers, contributing to the broader market rally.

articles.headline: Retail investors turn net sellers in FY26 after six years
articles.summary: Indian retail investors turned net sellers in the cash market in FY26, with net outflows of Rs 5,803 crore, according to a report by PL Capital. This reversal follows six years of consistent inflows and is attributed to high market valuations and profit booking amid global geopolitical uncertainties. Investors are becoming more selective in their picks.

articles.headline: ICICI Bank ownership shifts as FPIs trim their holdings
articles.summary: ICICI Bank saw a significant ownership shift in FY26, with Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) reducing their stake from 45.82% to 34.48%. Conversely, domestic mutual funds increased their holding to 32.08%, and domestic institutional investors rose to 27.83%. The shift reflects a growing presence of domestic capital in major Indian private banks.

articles.headline: Indian start-up deals drop 34% in 2025; funding down 18%
articles.summary: Indian start-ups raised $11.7 billion in 2025 across 1,632 deals, representing an 18% decline in funding value and a 34% drop in deal volume. The trend indicates a shift toward larger, late-stage funding rounds. Enterprise Applications and FinTech remained the top-funded sectors, while the number of new unicorns fell significantly compared to previous years.

articles.headline: West Asia conflict's second-round effects a concern: RBI Governor
articles.summary: RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted that the second-round effects of the West Asia conflict on global supply chains and commodity prices are a major concern for the Indian economy. He stated that while the immediate impact might be managed, prolonged disruptions could impact core inflation and require proactive monetary policy responses.

articles.headline: DMK and AIADMK face TVK challenge in TN polls
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu assembly elections are witnessing a three-way contest between the traditional DMK and AIADMK rivals and actor Vijay’s newly formed Tamil Vettri Kazhagam (TVK). The TVK is attracting significant support from younger voters, adding a layer of unpredictability to the election results, particularly in urban and industrial belts.

articles.headline: PM Modi using Central agencies to terrorise Opposition: Kharge
articles.summary: Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge accused PM Narendra Modi of using central agencies like the ED and IT Department to suppress political opponents. Speaking at a rally in Tamil Nadu, Kharge claimed these actions are intended to undermine the opposition before the elections and called for a united front to protect democratic values.

articles.headline: Centre proposes increasing sugar mill distance to 25 km
articles.summary: The Union government has proposed increasing the minimum distance between sugar mills from 15 km to 25 km to ensure adequate cane availability for existing units. State governments will decide on plant expansions based on local conditions. The move aims to prevent unhealthy competition and ensure the long-term viability of the sugar industry.

articles.headline: India's seafood exports rise 15% to Rs 72,325 crore
articles.summary: India's seafood exports grew by over 15% in value to reach Rs 72,325.82 crore in FY26, despite challenges in the US market. Export volume increased to 19.32 lakh tonnes. Growth was driven by strong demand for frozen shrimp and fish from China, the EU, and South-East Asia. The US remained the largest export destination for Indian seafood.

articles.headline: Delhi airport Pier C upgrade to boost international capacity
articles.summary: Delhi International Airport (DIAL) has converted Pier C at Terminal 3 from domestic to international operations, increasing international passenger capacity from 2 crore to 3.2 crore per year. The move aims to position Delhi as a major global aviation hub and improve connectivity for passengers transiting through West Asia and South-East Asia.

articles.headline: Glitches in licensing portal worry Indian pharma exporters
articles.summary: Indian pharmaceutical exporters are facing challenges with the CDSCO’s ONDLS portal, leading to delays in the issuance of critical export certificates. Industry bodies have raised concerns that these glitches could impact global trade and compliance with new WHO manufacturing norms. The government is urged to resolve these technical issues promptly.

articles.headline: SC seeks report on alleged clinical trial safety lapses
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the central government to file a detailed report on alleged shortcomings in the 2024 clinical trial rules. The order follows a PIL claiming inadequate safety provisions and oversight for participants in medical trials. The court is investigating whether existing rules sufficiently protect citizens from unethical practices.

articles.headline: Amara Raja crosses 1 GWh lithium storage milestone
articles.summary: Amara Raja Energy & Mobility has successfully deployed over one gigawatt-hour (GWh) of lithium-ion battery storage across 50,000 telecom sites in India. This milestone supports the company's expansion into sustainable energy storage solutions and establishes a foundation for its entry into other high-growth segments like data centers and EVs.

articles.headline: El Nino likely to emerge between May and July
articles.summary: Climate models indicate a high probability of an El Nino event emerging between May and July, which could impact the Indian monsoon and agricultural production. The Australian Bureau of Meteorology noted a transition from neutral to warmer-than-normal sea surface temperatures. This development adds a layer of uncertainty to India's agricultural outlook.

articles.headline: Cotton Corporation sells over 50% of 2025-26 produce
articles.summary: The state-run Cotton Corporation of India has sold more than 50 lakh bales of cotton, representing over half its procurement for the 2025-26 season. Strong demand from domestic mills and rising global prices have driven sales. The corporation’s cumulative sales have exceeded Rs 6,000 crore, reflecting a robust recovery in the cotton market.

articles.headline: Sony secures media rights for Asian Games 2026
articles.summary: Sony Pictures Networks India has acquired the media rights for the 2026 Asian Games to be held in Japan. The games will be broadcast live on Sony's sports channels and streamed on SonyLIV. This acquisition strengthens Sony’s portfolio of major international sporting events and its competitive position in the Indian media market.

articles.headline: Pakistan extends airspace curbs on Indian flights till May 24
articles.summary: Pakistan has extended the closure of its airspace for flights to and from India until May 24, citing security concerns following recent incidents in Kashmir. The move will continue to impact flight schedules and increase travel times for international passengers. Air carriers are making adjustments to their routes to minimize disruptions.

articles.headline: Shadow on Talks After Trump Hints at Iran Bombardment
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump's hints at potential bombardment of Iran if talks fail have stalled diplomatic progress. While Trump expressed a desire to resume negotiations, Iran has not committed to attending. Concurrently, US forces boarded an Iranian-sanctioned oil tanker in the Indian Ocean, further heightening regional tensions and impacting global shipping security through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Art of AI War: Sarvam to Boost Defence Prowess
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based Sarvam AI is collaborating with the defense ministry to establish a ₹300 crore Centre of Excellence (CoE) for indigenous AI applications. The project aims to reduce dependence on foreign AI models and strengthen sovereignty in military domains. Large-scale AI models will be developed for surveillance, reconnaissance, and battlefield decision-support systems.

articles.headline: Cabinet Secy Pushes No-communalism Code for Civil Servants
articles.summary: Cabinet Secretary TV Somanathan has urged civil servants to strictly avoid communal entanglements and maintain political neutrality. Addressing the 18th Civil Services Day, he emphasized that civil servants must offer frank, fearless advice to the executive even at the risk of displeasure. The code aims to preserve the foundational values of integrity and impartial administration.

articles.headline: Fairfax to Hike IIFL Stake Via Pref Allotment
articles.summary: Prem Watsa-backed Fairfax Financial Holdings is set to increase its stake in IIFL Capital through a preferential allotment. The deal, valued at approximately ₹1,000 crore, will see Fairfax raise its holding by about 10%. The move follows a due diligence process and signals a high-conviction bet on the growth of the Indian financial services platform.

articles.headline: Asset Declaration of Trinamool Nominee Under I-T Probe
articles.summary: The Income Tax department has launched an investigation into TMC nominee Debasish Kumar over alleged discrepancies in his asset declarations. Searches conducted on April 17 reportedly found ₹75 crore in assets, including gold and cash, which were not properly disclosed. A report has been submitted to the Election Commission for further action regarding the candidate's eligibility.

articles.headline: Junglemahal Contest: TMC and BJP Battle for Tribal Votes
articles.summary: In the Junglemahal region of West Bengal, comprising Purulia, Bankura, and Jhargram, the TMC and BJP are locked in a fierce contest. The BJP is focusing on development issues and anti-incumbency, while TMC highlights its welfare schemes like 'Lakshmir Bhandar.' The tribal vote, particularly the Kurmi community's demand for ST status, remains a critical factor in the 25 seats.

articles.headline: Muslim Leaders Party to Decision on Shamanur Grandson
articles.summary: In Karnataka, Muslim leaders within the Congress party are divided over the decision to field Shamanur Shanthajivappa's grandson in Davanagere. While some leaders expressed anguish over the lack of representation for the community, CM Siddaramaiah maintained the choice was based on winnability. The internal debate highlights the complexities of caste and religious representation in ticket distribution.

articles.headline: Congress Moves Breach of Privilege Notice Over PM’s Address
articles.summary: Congress MP KC Venugopal has moved a breach of privilege notice against PM Narendra Modi for his remarks during an address to the nation. The notice alleges that the PM made 'casting aspersions' on opposition MPs regarding the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill. Congress claims the address misrepresented the opposition's stance and attributed false motives to its members.

articles.headline: India Reviews Safety of its Ships After Hormuz Firing
articles.summary: The Indian government has conducted a comprehensive review of the safety of its merchant vessels following an Iranian firing incident in the Strait of Hormuz. Two Indian-flagged ships were identified as non-targets, and no fresh safety advisories have been issued yet. The Ministry of Shipping is monitoring the situation as tensions between Iran and Israel affect global trade routes.

articles.headline: Govt Pushes Chem Fert Alternatives After Output Slump
articles.summary: India is aggressively promoting chemical fertilizer alternatives, such as liquid nutrients and biofertilizers, following a 24.6% slump in production in March. The government aims for a 5% reduction in chemical fertilizer use in the 2026-27 season. Measures include targeting 100 high-use districts for immediate adoption of alternative soil nutrients to reduce the subsidy burden.

articles.headline: Palm Oil Imports may Increase Amid supply Constraints
articles.summary: Global palm oil supply is expected to remain constrained in FY27 due to biodiesel expansion in Malaysia and Indonesia. This could lead to a shift in India’s import patterns as prices rise. The crisis in West Asia has also impacted the import of sunflower and soy oils. India's annual palm oil production is about 82 mt, while imports range from 9-9.5 mt to meet domestic demand.

articles.headline: High Energy Prices to Strain India's Trade Balance: Moody's
articles.summary: Moody's Ratings warns that sustained high energy prices could strain India's trade balance and fiscal position. This may lead to uneven credit impacts across sectors like cement and chemicals. High input costs are expected to widen the trade deficit and pressure the rupee, though strong foreign reserves and infrastructure spending are expected to provide some cushion for the economy.

articles.headline: For Asian Paints, Green is a Worthful Way of Life
articles.summary: Asian Paints has integrated sustainability into its daily operations, focusing on energy, water, and waste management. The company has achieved 478% water replenishment in FY23 and aims to increase its use of renewable energy from 28% to 75% by 2030. These efforts are part of a broader strategy to ensure corporate responsibility and long-term business resilience.

articles.headline: ₹1,000 crore Worth of Mango Pulp Stuck in Warehouses
articles.summary: Mango processors in Andhra Pradesh are facing a crisis as approximately 2.5 lakh tonnes of mango pulp, valued at ₹1,000 crore, is stuck in warehouses. The situation is blamed on falling demand from the Middle East and Europe due to geopolitical tensions. Processors are seeking government intervention and financial support to manage high inventory costs and avoid spoilage.

articles.headline: Your Cola Can's Like a Fizz Out of Water
articles.summary: Soft drink manufacturers in India, including Coca-Cola and Pepsi, are facing a shortage of aluminum cans due to supply disruptions from the West Asia conflict. The crisis has led to a 25-30% premium on imported cans and a scarcity of Diet Coke and other canned beverages. Crisil reports that soft drink bottlers' margins could be hit by up to 250 bps due to rising packaging costs.

articles.headline: D-Street Bounces 1% as World Eager for Peace Pipe
articles.summary: Indian stock markets rebounded on Tuesday, with the Nifty closing above its 50-DMA and the Sensex rising 753 points to 79,273. The recovery was driven by a perceived de-escalation of the US-Iran conflict and a moderation in FPI selling. Market volatility, as measured by the VIX, fell 6.7%, though analysts remain cautious about the long-term impact of high energy prices and inflation.

articles.headline: HDFC Life Reappoints Vibha Padalkar as CEO for 5 Yrs
articles.summary: The board of HDFC Life Insurance has reappointed Vibha Padalkar as the Managing Director and CEO for a second five-year term starting September 12, 2026. The reappointment is subject to shareholder approval and regulatory clearance from IRDAI. Padalkar has led the company through significant growth and overseen its transition through the first major merger in the life insurance sector.

articles.headline: Taxman asks foreign buyers and NRIs to disclose source
articles.summary: The Income Tax department has asked offshore companies and non-resident Indians (NRIs) to explain the source of money used for acquiring shares in unlisted Indian companies. This move, part of a wider probe into tax evasion and money laundering, covers investments made between 2019-20 and 2022-23. Failure to provide satisfactory explanations could lead to reassessment of tax returns.

articles.headline: Ammonia Rule to Hurt Drugmakers and Pharma Supplies
articles.summary: A new fertiliser directive that restricts the sale of ammonia to the fertiliser sector is expected to create a raw material crunch for Indian drugmakers. Ammonia is a key input for several active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). The directive has led to immediate shortages and price hikes, potentially disrupting the production of life-saving medicines and impacting pharma exports.

articles.headline: Obesity Drug Mkt to Gain as New Players Enter
articles.summary: The global obesity drug market is set to expand with the entry of new oral GLP-1 medications from companies in India, France, and China. These new products are expected to make treatment more affordable and convenient compared to existing injectable drugs. Several Indian companies are partnering with global firms to develop and market these drugs, targeting a rapidly growing patient base.

articles.headline: Pentagon Wants $750B Wishlist in $1.5T Budget Request
articles.summary: The Pentagon has unveiled its $1.5 trillion budget request for FY27, which includes a $750 billion 'wishlist' for advanced military technologies. The proposal focuses on missile defense, drone dominance, and AI-driven systems to counter emerging threats. The budget request reflects a shift towards next-generation warfare and a commitment to maintaining US military superiority globally.

articles.headline: Oil Touches $100 on Uncertainty Over West Asia
articles.summary: Global oil prices reached $100 per barrel following uncertainty over a ceasefire in West Asia and threats to shipping routes in the Strait of Hormuz. US President Donald Trump warned of potential strikes against Iran if peace talks fail, leading to market volatility. The high prices are expected to impact global inflation and trade balances, particularly for energy-importing nations like India.

articles.headline: Amazon Strikes $30M Carbon Credit Deal with Rice Alliance
articles.summary: Amazon has signed a $30 million carbon credit deal with the Rice Alliance to buy carbon credits from Indian rice farmers. The deal aims to promote sustainable farming practices, such as direct-seeded rice and better water management, to reduce methane emissions. This is one of the largest agricultural carbon credit deals and will help Amazon meet its global sustainability goals.

articles.headline: Meity Adds Stricter AI Labelling Rule for Platforms
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (Meity) has introduced stricter rules for labelling AI-generated content on digital platforms. The new rules require platforms to clearly label any AI-generated text, images, or videos to prevent the spread of misinformation and deepfakes. This move is part of a broader effort to regulate AI and ensure the safety and trust of users online.

articles.headline: Infosys Set to Discuss CEO Transition After Salil Parekh
articles.summary: The board of Infosys is set to discuss its CEO transition plan as Salil Parekh's second five-year term ends in March 2027. While Parekh is eligible for another term, the board is looking at potential successors to ensure a smooth transition. The move follows recent management changes at other major IT firms and reflects the company's focus on long-term leadership stability and growth.

articles.headline: Bayern Munich Wins 13th Bundesliga Title in 14 Years
articles.summary: Bayern Munich secured its 13th Bundesliga title in 14 years after a record-breaking season. The team’s dominant performance was led by star forward Harry Kane, who broke the record for most goals in a single season. The victory cements Bayern's position as the most successful club in German football history and sets the stage for its pursuit of more trophies in European competitions.

articles.headline: Iran trip to Pak put on hold after Tehran snubs US terms
articles.summary: A high-level Iranian delegation's visit to Pakistan was postponed following failed negotiations with the US over maritime security in the Persian Gulf. Tensions escalated as the US imposed fresh sanctions on Iranian entities and seized a tanker carrying Iranian oil in the Bay of Bengal, citing violations of international energy trade restrictions.

articles.headline: Two accused locals could have averted April 22 terror attack
articles.summary: Security agencies reported that two local residents in Pahalgam sheltered three Pakistani terrorists for 24 hours before a deadly attack one year ago. Authorities state that timely information from these locals could have prevented the tragedy, which claimed several lives. The investigation revealed the duo provided logistical support and a hideout near the attack site.

articles.headline: Supreme Court: Non-believer has no right to challenge temple customs
articles.summary: Justice BV Nagarathna of the Supreme Court observed that individuals who do not believe in a specific religion or its tenets lack the legal standing to challenge its customs in court. The observation came during a hearing on Sabarimala temple traditions, emphasizing that constitutional religious protections are intrinsically tied to individual faith and belief systems.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh raises job quota for locals to 95%
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government has increased the job reservation for local candidates to 95% for all direct recruitments. This move follows a notification under Article 371(D) of the Constitution and applies to government jobs across all 26 districts. The policy aims to maximize employment opportunities for residents in their home districts and regional centers.

articles.headline: Bengaluru's two-wheeler population set to hit 1 crore by 2029
articles.summary: Transport department data projects that Bengaluru will have 1 crore registered two-wheelers by 2029, up from the current 66 lakh. The surge is driven by a lack of last-mile connectivity and rising demand for personal mobility. Officials noted that 1,400 new two-wheelers are added to the city's roads daily, contributing significantly to traffic congestion.

articles.headline: Lifestyle diseases surge in India as infectious ailments fall
articles.summary: A National Statistics Office report indicates a major shift in India's health landscape, with lifestyle diseases like cardiovascular issues and diabetes rising to 25.6% of reported cases. Conversely, deaths from infectious diseases have declined by 31% over the last decade. The report highlights that cardiovascular conditions now account for nearly one-fourth of all non-injury ailments.

articles.headline: Devenahalli to transform HAL airport into mega aviation hub
articles.summary: The state government has approved a 10-year master plan to integrate the HAL airport in Bengaluru into a massive aviation and aerospace hub. The project includes R&D centers, aircraft maintenance units, and specialized manufacturing facilities. Officials expect the hub to attract global aerospace firms and generate over $20 billion in economic output over the next decade.

articles.headline: HCLTech Q4 revenue rises 4.7%, flags deflationary guidance for FY25
articles.summary: HCL Technologies reported a 4.7% increase in revenue for the fourth quarter, reaching Rs 28,499 crore. However, the company issued a cautious revenue guidance of 3% to 5% for the next fiscal year, citing a deflationary environment and reduced discretionary spending by global clients in the telecom and financial services sectors.

articles.headline: Supreme Court rejects maintenance plea if DNA proves non-paternity
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a husband is not liable to pay maintenance for a child if a DNA test definitively proves he is not the biological father. The court emphasized that the legal presumption of legitimacy during marriage can be rebutted by conclusive scientific evidence. The ruling overturned a High Court order that had granted maintenance despite DNA test results.

articles.headline: Lightning deaths account for 56% of natural disaster fatalities in Karnataka
articles.summary: Official data reveals that lightning has become the deadliest natural disaster in Karnataka, causing 193 deaths in the last fiscal year alone. This accounts for over half of all natural disaster fatalities in the state. Experts cited climate variability and lack of lightning conductors on rural buildings as key factors for the high mortality rate, especially among farm laborers.

articles.headline: Bengaluru water tanker demand rises 25% amid heatwave
articles.summary: Water tanker demand in Bengaluru has surged by 25% as groundwater levels hit a record low this summer. The BWSSB reported that over 15,700 tanker orders were fulfilled in the last two months, worth Rs 1.7 crore. Many residential complexes are now entirely dependent on private tankers, leading to price hikes of up to Rs 500 per load in some areas.

articles.headline: NTA focuses on 'stress-free' and zero-error exams, says Union Minister
articles.summary: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan announced that the National Testing Agency (NTA) is moving toward a 'stress-free' examination model with a focus on zero-error results. The initiative includes global benchmarking of entrance exams and the introduction of computer-based testing to eliminate human errors and leaks, particularly for high-stakes exams like JEE and NEET.

articles.headline: Two more tigers found dead in Madhya Pradesh's Panna and Kanha
articles.summary: Forest officials in Madhya Pradesh reported the deaths of two adult tigers in Panna and Kanha reserves, bringing the state's tiger death toll to 23 this year. Initial investigations suggest one tiger died in a territorial fight, while the other's cause of death is being verified through a post-mortem. The rising fatalities have prompted concerns over habitat management.

articles.headline: Meta to use employee mouse clicks and keystrokes to train AI
articles.summary: Tech giant Meta has announced plans to use internal employee data, including mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes, to train its next generation of artificial intelligence models. The goal is to create AI that can better understand human-computer interaction and perform complex workplace tasks. Privacy advocates have raised concerns over the level of workplace surveillance involved.

articles.headline: Clock Ticks on US-Iran Ceasefire as Talks Remain Uncertain
articles.summary: Pakistan is working to mediate a second round of ceasefire talks between the US and Iran as a two-week truce expires. US Vice President JD Vance cancelled a trip to Pakistan as Iran remains undecided on participation. Tensions rose as US forces boarded the tanker MT Tifani in the Indian Ocean, while the US imposed fresh sanctions on Iran-linked entities for blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: BJP Files EC Complaint After Kharge Calls PM Modi a Terrorist
articles.summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge sparked a political row in Chennai by calling PM Narendra Modi a “terrorist.” Kharge later clarified he meant the PM was “terrorising” political parties. The BJP condemned the remark as an insult to 1.4 billion Indians and filed a complaint with the Election Commission, alleging a violation of the model code of conduct and defamatory behaviour.

articles.headline: John Ternus to Succeed Tim Cook as Apple CEO in September
articles.summary: Apple has named John Ternus as its next CEO, taking over from Tim Cook on 1 September. Cook, who led the firm since 2011 and saw its value exceed $4 trillion, will become executive chairman. The move signals a strategic bet on hardware engineering and integrated devices as AI transforms the tech industry. India is expected to remain central to Apple's manufacturing and market strategies.

articles.headline: Govt Rejects Security Nod for Noida Airport CEO
articles.summary: The Bureau of Civil Aviation Security (BCAS) has rejected the security clearance for Christoph Schnellmann, the CEO of Noida International Airport. The rejection is based on a 2011 order that mandates the airport security coordinator must be an Indian national.
