This decision could delay the implementation of security measures and official commencement of flight operations at the site.

articles.headline: Meity Proposes Continuous Watermarks for AI-Generated Media Content
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (Meity) issued a draft amendment requiring AI-modified images and videos to display clear labels throughout their duration. The move targets platforms that use fleeting or obscured markers. Social media giants like Instagram, X, and YouTube must ensure labels are prominently visible to help users identify deepfakes and manipulated media.

articles.headline: Japan Lifts Ban on Export of Lethal Weapons
articles.summary: Japan has officially lifted its post-war ban on the export of lethal weapons to strengthen its domestic defense industry and deepen ties with security partners. The new guidelines allow for the export of fighter jets, missiles, and destroyers to 17 countries with existing defense agreements. Critics warn the move violates Japan's pacifist constitution and could escalate global tensions.

articles.headline: India-US Trade Talks Focus on Tariffs and Framework Direction
articles.summary: Negotiators from India and the US are meeting in Washington to discuss the future of their trading relationship following the US Supreme Court's invalidation of previous reciprocal tariff plans. India aims to resolve Section 301 exposure before new punitive levies are announced. The talks include potential reductions in tariffs for energy and technology sectors to boost bilateral trade.

articles.headline: India’s Crude Oil Imports from Middle East Slump Amid War
articles.summary: India's crude oil imports fell 13% in March as the conflict in West Asia halted shipments via the Strait of Hormuz. To compensate, refiners doubled imports from Russia to 2.25 million barrels per day. The share of Middle Eastern oil in India’s total imports dropped to a record low of 26.3%, with shipments from Iraq and the UAE reaching multi-year lows due to maritime security risks.

articles.headline: Abhishek Sharma Smashes 135 as SRH Defeat Delhi Capitals
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad opener Abhishek Sharma hit an unbeaten 135 off 68 balls, including 10 sixes, to power his team to 242/2 against Delhi Capitals. Despite a valiant 57 from Nitish Rana, DC were restricted to 195/9, with SRH bowler Eshan Malinga taking 4/32. The 47-run victory maintains SRH’s strong position in the IPL standings, moving them to third place.

articles.headline: Amazon Invests Additional $5 Billion in AI Startup Anthropic
articles.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is investing an additional $5 billion in Anthropic PBC, with the potential to reach $20 billion over time. The deal deepens ties in the competitive AI industry, as Anthropic plans to spend over $100 billion over the next decade on Amazon’s cloud technologies and specialized chips. The move intensifies the battle for dominance in generative artificial intelligence.

articles.headline: EQT Raises $15.6 Billion for Asia Buyout Fund
articles.summary: Swedish private equity firm EQT has raised $15.6 billion for its latest Asia-focused buyout fund, BPEA IX. This is one of the largest funds raised for the region and will target high-growth sectors such as technology, healthcare, and industrial technology. The fund attracted strong interest from both existing and new investors, reflecting confidence in the long-term potential of Asian markets.

articles.headline: Suzlon Re-enters Europe Mkt with New Wind Turbines
articles.summary: Indian wind energy company Suzlon has announced its re-entry into the European market with its new 3 MW turbine platform. The move is part of the company's global expansion strategy and follows its successful turnaround in the domestic market. Suzlon aims to leverage its new technology to tap into the growing demand for renewable energy in Europe and other international markets.

articles.headline: Nestle India Reports 26% Profit Jump on Strong Volume Growth
articles.summary: Nestle India posted a 26% rise in standalone net profit to ₹1,114 crore for the March quarter. Revenue from operations grew 22.6% to ₹6,747.7 crore, marked by the strongest sales growth in a decade. Performance was driven by double-digit volume gains in confectionery and beverages, alongside broad-based demand and expanded rural reach to 216,000 villages.

articles.headline: Sebi Panel Recommends NSE Pay $193 Million to Settle Past Cases
articles.summary: An external panel has recommended that the National Stock Exchange (NSE) pay over ₹1,800 crore to settle pending legal disputes with Sebi. The settlement would resolve long-running allegations of governance lapses and unfair access. Resolution is a critical step toward NSE’s planned initial public offering, which has been delayed for nearly a decade. Demand letters are expected soon.

articles.headline: AB InBev Named Target of Anti-Trust Cartel Probe in Telangana
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has made Anheuser-Busch InBev a target of a cartel investigation involving 42 alcohol retailers in Telangana. The probe alleges a cartel was formed to exclude AB InBev's rivals, leading to an unfair surge in market share. AB InBev has obtained a temporary court injunction as the agency investigates allegations of anti-competitive behavior.

articles.headline: HCLTech Q4 Net Profit Up 4.2% YoY to ₹4,488cr
articles.summary: HCLTech reported a 4.2% year-on-year increase in net profit to ₹4,488 crore for the fourth quarter. However, the company provided a cautious revenue growth guidance of 1-4% for FY27, citing macro-economic uncertainties and a slowdown in tech spending. The management highlighted the impact of AI-led transformations and its focus on cloud and engineering services for future growth.

articles.headline: SBI Seeks Supreme Court Review of Spectrum Asset Status
articles.summary: State Bank of India (SBI) has filed a review petition in the Supreme Court against a February ruling regarding telecom spectrum. The court had ruled that spectrum cannot be treated as a proprietary asset in insolvency proceedings. SBI argues the decision contains errors and warns of far-reaching negative consequences for lenders and the telecom sector's financial stability.

articles.headline: Five Lakh New PNG Connections Issued as Consumers Shift
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum announced that 5.01 lakh new Piped Natural Gas (PNG) connections have been issued since March as consumers move away from LPG. About 39,400 consumers surrendered LPG cylinders after receiving PNG. The government is promoting PNG to ensure fuel security and ease the pressure on LPG supplies caused by regional geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: Listed Firms' CSR Spending Increases 23% to Rs 22,212 Crore
articles.summary: Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) spending by firms listed on the NSE mainboard rose 23% in FY25 to Rs 22,212 crore. The surge followed a 22% increase in average net profits over the last three years. Of the 2,142 listed firms, 1,549 were required to spend on CSR. The data indicates strong compliance with the mandate to spend 2% of average net profits on social causes.

articles.headline: Shah Warns 'Infiltrators', Attacks Mamata Banerjee in Bengal
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah, during an election rally, vowed to protect West Bengal borders from illegal infiltrators and 'goons.' He criticized Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee for alleged misgovernance and promised to seal international borders with barbed fencing within 45 days if BJP wins. He also addressed the Gorkha issue, promising a permanent political solution.

articles.headline: RBI Brings Recurring Card, Digital Payments Under E-mandate
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has updated e-mandate rules for recurring electronic payments via cards, prepaid instruments, and UPI. The new framework requires financial institutions to notify customers at least 24 hours before a transaction. It aims to minimise digital fraud while providing facilities for automatic replenishment of balances for services like FASTag and NCMC.

articles.headline: Fireworks Unit Blast in Thrissur Dist Kills 13
articles.summary: A massive explosion at a firework manufacturing unit in Mundathicode, Thrissur district, Kerala, resulted in 13 deaths and several injuries. At least 13 people were killed instantly, and 13 others are undergoing treatment, with five in critical condition. The blast occurred during preparations for the upcoming Thrissur Pooram festival, prompting an investigation into safety lapses.

articles.headline: India and South Korea agree to broaden phase-3 defense cooperation
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol have signed an agreement to expand defense ties. The Phase-3 cooperation involves co-development of advanced missile systems, self-propelled artillery like the K9 Vajra, and anti-aircraft guns. The deal includes significant technology transfers to bolster India's domestic defense manufacturing sector.

articles.headline: FDA directs Maharashtra food outlets to disclose cheese substitutes
articles.summary: The Maharashtra Food and Drug Administration has set an April 30 deadline for eateries and food suppliers to clearly label use of 'cheese analogues' or vegetable oil-based substitutes. Failure to disclose these substitutes on menus and display boards will attract penalties under the Food Safety and Standards Act. The move follows complaints about deceptive labeling of dairy products.
